task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application window is visible, specifically showing the 'Roman Numerals' activity interface.

task_completion.description: At least 5 conversion problems are solved in the GCompris activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing progress in the GCompris activity, such as a progress bar indicating 5 or more completed levels/problems, or multiple distinct screenshots showing different solved problems.

task_completion.description: A text file named 'roman_guide.txt' is created in the Documents folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor window (e.g., gedit, nano, mousepad) or terminal is visible showing the creation or editing of a file named 'roman_guide.txt' located in '~/Documents/'.

task_completion.description: The required header is present in the text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'Roman Numeral Reference' is visible at the top of the text document.

task_completion.description: The 7 standard Roman numeral symbols and their correct values are listed in the text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text document contains the symbols I, V, X, L, C, D, M paired with their respective values 1, 5, 10, 50, 100, 500, 1000 (e.g., 'I = 1', 'V - 5', etc.).

task_completion.description: A brief sentence describing the conversion tasks is present in the text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A sentence in the text document that mentions converting numbers to Roman numerals (or vice versa), reflecting the activity played in GCompris.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual GCompris application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GCompris GUI running the Roman Numerals activity, not just a web browser searching for Roman numerals or a terminal script.

integrity.description: The creation or editing of the text file is visible in the screenshots.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (GUI or terminal) is shown with the contents of the cheat sheet being drafted, proving the agent generated it during the episode rather than it magically appearing.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply copy-paste a pre-formatted block of text from an external source.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file appears to be manually typed or formatted by the agent, matching the specific requested structure (header, list, sentence) in a natural way.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct Roman numeral values are: I = 1, V = 5, X = 10, L = 50, C = 100, D = 500, M = 1000. The expected file path is '~/Documents/roman_guide.txt'. The required header is exactly 'Roman Numeral Reference'.
